title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Claim Audit: Test Layer Strategy
summary: A product passes a familiar unit test but trusts input, overcollects data, leaks failure details, loses work, and has no threat or recovery evidence. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 test layer strategy increment in trust and quality assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]

release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-test-layer-strategy — Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: pp1-test-layer-strategy
decision-evidence: Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
